name: SOC Analyst Training Lab
description: Security Operations Center analyst training environment with enterprise SIEM, network monitoring, and simulated attack scenarios. Students practice log analysis, alert triage, threat hunting, and incident response workflows.

objectives.title: Log Analysis
objectives.description: Analyze Windows Event Logs, Sysmon logs, and web server access logs in Splunk to identify suspicious activity patterns across the environment.
hints: Search for Event IDs 4624, 4625, and 4672 in Windows Security logs.
Look for unusual user-agent strings and HTTP status codes in web server logs.

objectives.title: Alert Triage
objectives.description: Triage and prioritize security alerts generated by Security Onion IDS/IPS, classifying each as true positive, false positive, or benign.
hints: Review Suricata alerts in Security Onion's Kibana interface.
Cross-reference alerts with known network baselines to identify false positives.

objectives.title: Malware Detection
objectives.description: Detect and identify malware artifacts on the Windows workstation using endpoint telemetry, Sysmon logs, and SIEM correlation rules.
hints: Look for suspicious process creation chains in Sysmon Event ID 1.
Check for unusual network connections from the workstation using Sysmon Event ID 3.

objectives.title: Incident Timeline
objectives.description: Reconstruct a complete incident timeline from initial compromise through lateral movement to data exfiltration using log correlation.
hints: Correlate events across multiple log sources using timestamps.
Map activities to MITRE ATT&CK techniques for each phase of the attack.

objectives.title: IOC Extraction
objectives.description: Extract all Indicators of Compromise including IP addresses, domain names, file hashes, and registry keys associated with the attack.
hints: Use Splunk queries to extract unique external IP addresses from firewall logs.
Check VirusTotal or similar threat intel feeds for identified file hashes.

objectives.title: Threat Hunting
objectives.description: Proactively hunt for additional compromise indicators and persistence mechanisms that were not detected by automated alerts.
hints: Hunt for scheduled tasks and services created during the incident timeframe.
Look for encoded PowerShell commands in process creation logs.

objectives.title: Write Incident Report
objectives.description: Compile a formal incident report including executive summary, technical findings, IOCs, MITRE ATT&CK mapping, and remediation recommendations.
hints: Follow NIST SP 800-61 incident handling guidelines for report structure.
Include all extracted IOCs and recommended detection rules.
